name: genuinely_good
original_prompt: In two or three sentences, explain why ice floats on water.
llm_output: Ice floats because water is unusual: when it freezes, its molecules arrange into a crystal lattice that holds them slightly farther apart than they are in liquid water. This makes ice about 9% less dense than the liquid, so it floats. That same expansion is why water pipes can burst in winter.
expected: A correct, well-reasoned, complete answer. All three critics should score high (>=4) with high confidence and no real issues, ideally triggering the unanimous-pass short-circuit (adjudicated=False).
